(g). , (2), redesignated subsec. (h) as (g) and struck out former subsec. (g) which related to specific allocations.Pub. L. 105–178, § 4003(g)(1)Subsec. (h). , redesignated subsec. (j) as (h). Text read as follows: “The Secretary shall prescribe regulations to develop an improved formula and process for allocating amounts made available for grants under among States eligible for those amounts. In prescribing those regulations, the Secretary shall—Pub. L. 105–178, § 4003(g)(3)section 31102(a) of this title“(1) consider ways to provide incentives to States that demonstrate innovative, successful, cost-efficient, or cost-effective programs to promote commercial motor vehicle safety and hazardous material transportation safety;“(2) place special emphasis on incentives to States that conduct traffic safety enforcement activities that are coupled with motor carrier safety inspections; and“(3) consider ways to provide incentives to States that increase compatibility of State commercial motor vehicle safety and hazardous material transportation regulations with Government safety regulations and promote other factors intended to promote effectiveness and efficiency the Secretary decides are appropriate.”Subsec. children.heading: Reimbursement and Payment to Recipients for Government Share of Costs.—
children.label: b Reimbursement and Payment to Recipients for Government Share of Costs.—

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Amounts made available under subsection (a) shall be used to reimburse financial assistance recipients proportionally for the Federal Government’s share of the costs incurred.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: The Secretary shall reimburse a recipient, in accordance with a financial assistance agreement made under section 31102 (except subsection ()(5) of that section), 31103, or 31313, an amount that is at least 85 percent of the costs incurred by the recipient in a fiscal year in developing and implementing programs under such sections.l

children.heading: .—Commercial motor vehicle enforcement training and support grant program
children.label: ii .—Commercial motor vehicle enforcement training and support grant program
children.text: The Secretary shall reimburse a recipient, in accordance with a financial assistance agreement made under section 31102()(5), an amount that is equal to 100 percent of the costs incurred by the recipient in a fiscal year in developing and implementing a training program under that section.l

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: B .—Limitation
children.text: The Secretary shall pay the recipient an amount not more than the Federal Government share of the total costs approved by the Federal Government in the financial assistance agreement.

children.heading: .—In-kind contributions
children.label: C .—In-kind contributions
children.text: The Secretary shall include a recipient’s in-kind contributions in determining the reimbursement.

children.heading: .—Vouchers
children.label: 3 .—Vouchers
children.text: Each recipient shall submit vouchers at least quarterly for costs the recipient incurs in developing and implementing programs under sections 31102, 31103, and 31313.

children.heading: .—Deductions for Program Support
children.label: c .—Deductions for Program Support
children.text: On October 1 of each fiscal year, or as soon after that date as practicable, the Secretary may deduct from amounts made available under paragraphs (1), (2), and (5) of subsection (a) for that fiscal year not more than 1.50 percent of those amounts for program support in that fiscal year.

children.heading: .—Grants and Cooperative Agreements as Contractual Obligations
children.label: d .—Grants and Cooperative Agreements as Contractual Obligations
children.text: The approval of a financial assistance agreement by the Secretary under section 31102, 31103, or 31313 is a contractual obligation of the Federal Government for payment of the Federal Government’s share of costs in carrying out the provisions of the grant or cooperative agreement.

children.heading: .—Eligible Activities
children.label: e .—Eligible Activities
children.text: The Secretary shall establish criteria for eligible activities to be funded with financial assistance agreements under this section and publish those criteria in a notice of funding availability before the financial assistance program application period.

children.heading: .—Period of Availability of Financial Assistance Agreement Funds for Recipient Expenditures
children.label: f .—Period of Availability of Financial Assistance Agreement Funds for Recipient Expenditures

children.text: For grants made for carrying out section 31102, other than section 31102(), for the fiscal year in which the Secretary approves the financial assistance agreement and for the next 2 fiscal years.l

For grants made or cooperative agreements entered into for carrying out section 31102()(2), for the fiscal year in which the Secretary approves the financial assistance agreement and for the next 2 fiscal years.l
For grants made for carrying out section 31102()(3), for the fiscal year in which the Secretary approves the financial assistance agreement and for the next 4 fiscal years.l
For grants made for carrying out section 31102()(5), for the fiscal year in which the Secretary approves the financial assistance agreement and for the next 4 fiscal years.l
For grants made for carrying out section 31103, for the fiscal year in which the Secretary approves the financial assistance agreement and for the next 2 fiscal years.

For grants made or cooperative agreements entered into for carrying out section 31313, for the fiscal year in which the Secretary approves the financial assistance agreement and for the next 4 fiscal years.

children.heading: .—Contract Authority; Initial Date of Availability
children.label: g .—Contract Authority; Initial Date of Availability
children.text: Amounts authorized from the Highway Trust Fund (other than the Mass Transit Account) by this section shall be available for obligation on the date of their apportionment or allocation or on October 1 of the fiscal year for which they are authorized, whichever occurs first.

children.heading: .—Availability of Funding
children.label: h .—Availability of Funding
children.text: Amounts made available under this section shall remain available until expended.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), amounts not expended by a recipient during the period of availability shall be released back to the Secretary for reallocation for any purpose under section 31102, 31103, or 31313 or this section to ensure, to the maximum extent possible, that all such amounts are obligated.

children.heading: .—Motor carrier safety assistance program
children.label: 2 .—Motor carrier safety assistance program
children.text: Amounts made available for the motor carrier safety assistance program established under section 31102 (other than amounts made available to carry out section 31102()) that are not expended by a recipient during the period of availability shall be released back to the Secretary for reallocation under that program.l
